reason: The text contains God's pronouncement of significant supernatural consequences that will affect humanity collectively, not just individuals. Specifically: (1) The curse upon the ground (אֲרוּרָ֤ה הָֽאֲדָמָה) affecting all future labor and subsistence; (2) The multiplication of pain and suffering for the woman affecting childbirth; (3) The pronouncement of death and return to dust for Adam and all mankind; (4) The introduction of thorns, thistles, and toil as permanent conditions. These are not individual destructions but rather supernatural judgments by Yahweh that reshape the human and natural condition going forward, affecting the general public (all of humanity and creation). The consequences are presented as 'upcoming' from the narrative's temporal perspective—conditions that will be realized in the future experience of mankind.
